column32: No confirmed large-scale named corporate AI center (e.g., Amazon, Google, Microsoft on-campus AI lab) identified beyond the Korea-government partnership","Comprehensive first-time research conducted March 29, 2026. P1 COMMITMENT (25/27): NYU has a very strong and well-documented AI governance and leadership structure. Clay Shirky holds the title of Vice Provost for AI and Technology in Education. Andrea Hernandez is the AI Governance and Strategy Director in the President's Office. The AI Leadership Council (AILC) is a standing governance body reporting to the President, Provost, and EVP twice a year. The NYU AI Center of Excellence (AICoE) serves as the centralized enterprise hub for scalable AI solutions. In November 2025, NYU created the Courant Institute School of Mathematics, Computing, and Data Science -- a historic structural move described explicitly as a 'major advance in our strategic commitment to science and technology.' President Mills, Provost Dopico, and EVP de Pablo jointly signed this announcement. No separately published multi-year AI strategic plan document was located, which limits the maximum P1 score slightly. P2 CURRICULUM (27/30): NYU offers an extensive AI-integrated curriculum. NYU Tandon offers the MS in Emerging Technologies (on-campus and online) with a dedicated ML and AI concentration; Fall 2026 applications are open. NYU Stern offers the MS in Business Analytics and AI (MSBAi). NYU SPS offers AI and technology degrees and an 'AI Readiness: Strategic Skills for Professionals' course. CDS offers world's first MS in Data Science. The Courant Institute School now houses CS, CDS, and Math under one roof. Cross-disciplinary AI integration is evident at Stern (Fubon Center), Silver School of Social Work (C+M Center, McSilver AI Hub), and across engineering. No evidence that a universal AI literacy requirement applies to all undergraduates, which limits the maximum P2 score. P3 PARTNERSHIPS (18/20): NYU is a founding member of the $500M+ Empire AI Consortium with compute access via the NVIDIA Blackwell-powered Empire AI Beta supercomputer. The Global AI Frontier Lab (opened September 2024, Advisory Council inaugural meeting January 27, 2026) is a formal government-to-university partnership between NYU and South Korea's MSIT/IITP. The AI Innovation Accelerator Program (AIIA) MOU was signed February 2025 with NIPA/IITP. The AI Now Institute is affiliated with NYU Tandon and CDS. The NYU Center on International Cooperation produces AI governance policy work. No major named commercial on-campus AI lab (e.g., a dedicated Google or Microsoft AI center at NYU) was confirmed, preventing a perfect P3 score. P4 RESEARCH (15/16): NYU's AI research ecosystem is world-class. Faculty include Turing Award winner Yann LeCun (2025 Queen Elizabeth Prize), Kyunghyun Cho (NeurIPS 2025 keynote, ICML 2025 best paper), Julia Stoyanovich (PECASE, Center for Responsible AI), Ernest Davis (2025 AAAI Fellow), and many more with NSF CAREER and Sloan awards. NYU authors produced 127 papers at NeurIPS 2025 and 88 at NeurIPS 2024. Research institutes include CDS, CILVR Lab, Global AI Frontier Lab, Center for Responsible AI, AI4CE Lab, ML2 NLP group, Agile Robotics and Perception Lab, Fubon Data Analytics and AI Initiative. Empire AI Consortium provides shared GPU compute. The only evidence gap is a lack of detailed public disclosure on NYU's own on-premises GPU cluster specifics, so one point is withheld. P5 ATTITUDE (7/7): NYU's posture is clearly ENABLING/PROGRESSIVE. Evidence: creation of a new school dedicated to computing and data science (Nov 2025); Vice Provost for AI role; AICoE; founding membership in Empire AI; active government international AI lab partnership; 127 NeurIPS 2025 papers; explicit statements by President Mills framing AI as a strategic priority. No resistance or cautious language detected.","1. NYU created the Courant Institute School of Mathematics, Computing, and Data Science on November 13, 2025 -- a new school merging Math, CDS, and CS departments. 2. Clay Shirky serves as Vice Provost for AI and Technology in Education; Andrea Hernandez is AI Governance and Strategy Director; the AI Leadership Council is a standing governance body. 3. NYU AI Center of Excellence (AICoE) is a centralized enterprise AI hub. 4. NYU is a founding member of Empire AI Consortium ($500M+ public-private); Empire AI Beta uses NVIDIA Blackwell platform. 5. Global AI Frontier Lab (NYU + South Korea MSIT/IITP) opened September 2024 at One MetroTech, co-directed by Yann LeCun and Kyunghyun Cho; Advisory Council held inaugural meeting January 27, 2026. 6. AI Innovation Accelerator MOU signed February 2025. 7. Yann LeCun awarded 2025 Queen Elizabeth Prize for Engineering. 8. 127 NYU author papers at NeurIPS 2025. 9. NYU Tandon MS in Emerging Technologies expanded with on-campus AI concentration for Fall 2026. 10. NYU ranks No. 1 in New York for research spending.",,"https://www.nyu.edu/life/information-technology/artificial-intelligence-at-nyu/governance.html

column51: No confirmed dedicated on-campus corporate AI R&D center (e.g., Amazon AI Lab or Meta AI lab physically located on campus)","COMPREHENSIVE FIRST-TIME RESEARCH COMPLETED AS OF 2026-03-29. P1 COMMITMENT (scored 25/27): U-M has exceptionally strong institutional AI commitment. Vision 2034 (now rebranded Look to Michigan) is a 10-year plan with AI and data science as one of seven named commitment areas, backed by a $1 billion/10-year investment. AIIM (AI Institutes at Michigan) was formally launched in March 2025 as a coordinating umbrella body. An AIIM AI Research Summit for Academic Leadership was convened to align all three campuses. President Grasso expanded Look to Michigan to include advanced technology as a fifth pillar. VP for Research Arthur Lupia and CIO Ravi Pendse serve as executive AI champions. The GenAI services portal (genai.umich.edu) is operational and campus-wide. One point deducted for absence of a singular university-level CAIO title and still-maturing AIIM governance structures. One point deducted for lack of a fully published formal AI governance policy document. P2 CURRICULUM (scored 26/30): U-M's curriculum footprint is very strong. UM-Dearborn offers MS in AI (4 concentrations). UM-Flint offers MS in AI (8 concentrations). Ann Arbor offers BS in CS with intelligent systems focus and MS/PhD with AI as primary theme. A new AI minor launched Winter 2026 for all disciplines university-wide. MIDAS Student Organizations Council has 1,100+ students. Michigan Online has 280+ open courses plus Google AI certificates for 66,500 students. CDAIO executive program launched June 2025. Responsible AI course available. Cross-disciplinary AI integration documented in Public Health, Medicine, Business, Ford School, Law, and Engineering. Deductions for: no standalone undergraduate AI major at Ann Arbor, no confirmed campus-wide mandatory AI literacy requirement. P3 PARTNERSHIPS (scored 19/20): Among the strongest partnership portfolios found. Key partnerships: (1) OpenAI NextGenAI consortium (February 24, 2026) - research funding, model access, compute; (2) LANL SPARC - $15M 5-year contract + $1.25B facility; (3) Google - 66,500 students' free AI certificates, GCP Goes to School, Google Public Sector agentic AI TA with 9,000 students; (4) Microsoft - U-M GenAI platform, MIDAS PODS partner; (5) Schmidt Sciences - $10M+ fellowship program, one of nine global universities; (6) State of Michigan $100M SSRP grant for LANL facility. Industry lab centers directly on campus not confirmed. One point deducted for no confirmed on-campus dedicated corporate AI lab facility. P4 RESEARCH (scored 15/16): Exceptional research infrastructure and output. Michigan AI Lab (ai.engin.umich.edu) directed by Rada Mihalcea. MIDAS with 570+ faculty affiliates. MICDE with 120+ engineering researchers. AI&DHI (merged with e-HAIL). TIERRA at Michigan Medicine. Annual research volume exceeds $2.04B. 18 papers at NeurIPS 2025; papers at EMNLP, ACL, NAACL, ICLR, CVPR 2025. NeurIPS 2024 Best Papers (Mihalcea/Jin). PECASE award (Koutra). NSF CAREER awards. New faculty hires in ML alignment, self-improving AI, responsible HCI/AI. Schmidt AI in Science fellows. Michigan Data Science Fellowship. $1.25B LANL HPC/AI facility in planning. NSF postdoctoral AI for science funding confirmed. PODS grants catalyzing $130M+ in external grants. One point deducted because the flagship LANL supercomputing facility is still in planning/pre-construction phase (no operational compute infrastructure yet). P5 ATTITUDE (scored 7/7): ENABLING/PROGRESSIVE. U-M's posture is unambiguously enabling. The university self-described as 'the first major university to offer a custom AI platform for its entire community.' Vision 2034 centers AI. AIIM launched proactively. Multiple responsible AI programs (TIERRA, MIDAS ethics work, STPP) demonstrate principled enablement rather than restriction. President Ono and President Grasso have both made public statements strongly championing AI. The Michigan Futures Initiative AI Moonshot sprint reflects proactive engagement with AI sustainability challenges rather than avoidance.","Vision 2034/Look to Michigan $1B strategic plan with AI as named pillar; AIIM launched March 2025 as centralized AI coordination body; OpenAI NextGenAI partnership February 24, 2026; $1.25B LANL HPC+AI facility (state-backed); New AI minor Winter 2026 open university-wide; MIDAS with 570+ faculty affiliates and $130M+ in catalyzed external grants from PODS; 18 NeurIPS 2025 papers; Google partnership (66,500 students); Schmidt AI in Science fellowship ($10M+); U-M GenAI platform (Microsoft-backed) serving entire campus; CDAIO executive program June 2025; AI&DHI+e-HAIL merged health AI hub active March 2026; Michigan Futures Initiative AI Moonshot Sprint October 2025.",,https://record.umich.edu/articles/u-m-launches-effort-to-advance-ai-research-collaboration/

column31: No publicly confirmed stand-alone AI PhD program; AI doctoral students work within existing PhD programs in CS/ECE/Engineering","Comprehensive first-time research completed March 29, 2026. P1 COMMITMENT (25/27): Northeastern scores near maximum on commitment. The university appointed its inaugural Chief of Artificial Intelligence (Javed Aslam, Khoury professor) in October 2024, seated in the Provost's office with explicit university-wide scope. An AI Council of institutional leaders meets weekly. The 'Experience Unleashed' academic plan succeeded the AI-centric 'Northeastern 2025' plan. A CPS Associate Dean for AI Initiatives exists, and CATLR runs a structured AI Fellows/Scholars/Instructional Assistants program for 2025-2026. Deductions: no single publicly published AI governance policy document found; AI Council's charter not independently published. P2 CURRICULUM (27/30): Exceptionally strong. MS in AI offered at Boston (Khoury + CoE joint), Silicon Valley, and Portland (Roux). The program is interdisciplinary with concentrations in ML, computer vision, robotics, energy systems, continuous process engineering, bioengineering, and health data. An undergraduate Interdisciplinary AI minor is available across colleges, emphasizing ethics and real-world application. An Information Ethics minor covers AI ethics. EAI (Enterprise AI) graduate course catalog covers AI applications, responsible AI, healthcare/finance/HR AI, data governance, and applied ML. Online Graduate Certificate in AI Applications targets working professionals. AI for Impact co-op program embeds students in public-interest AI work. Khoury's three-pillar approach (technical, applied, social/ethical) is institutionalized. Score deductions: no mandatory university-wide AI literacy requirement for all students confirmed. P3 PARTNERSHIPS (18/20): Anthropic partnership (April 2025) as first U.S. higher ed partner is landmark; Claude for Education deployed enterprise-wide. CRAIG (NSF-funded, Nov 2025) brings Meta, Nationwide, Honda Research, CISCO, Worthington Steel, and Bread Financial. Roux Institute has 300+ industry partners including L.L.Bean, WEX, MassHealth, Bangor Savings, Idexx, Jackson Labs. Massachusetts AI Hub engagement with faculty winning innovation challenges. InnovateMA government partnership via Burnes Center co-ops. Broad AI Solutions Hub with industry clients. Deductions: Anthropic partnership details on exclusivity/financial scope not fully public; CRAIG is multi-university, reducing exclusive credit. P4 RESEARCH (15/16): EAI has 90+ faculty (62 core, 31 affiliate) across disciplines. NSF $9M NDIF grant led by Northeastern. NSF-funded CRAIG launched 2025. Massachusetts AI Hub AI Models Challenge winners (2 faculty, Oct 2025). Multiple AI research labs: SMILE Lab, UbiWell Lab, SEAL, Relational Agents Group, AI-EDGE NSF Institute participation, CTML seminar series. HPC infrastructure: Explorer cluster upgraded to 32 H200 GPUs (2+ PetaFLOPS, 2.5x expansion) at MGHPCC; AICR cluster incoming summer 2026. Score deductions: No confirmed dedicated on-campus AI supercomputer; compute still consortium-shared at MGHPCC; AICR not yet live. P5 ATTITUDE (7/7): Progressive/enabling posture is unambiguous. 'World's leading AI-enabled university' declared goal. column31: Emerging pressure from federal grant environment may constrain research capacity if private-sector alternatives (BAIR Grant, Lightspeed) do not scale to meet historical federal funding levels [NEW 2026-03-29]","Comprehensive first-time research conducted March 29, 2026 via 10 targeted web searches covering: (1) UC Berkeley AI institutional readiness and strategy 2025-2026; (2) UC Berkeley AI office and executive leadership; (3) BAIR and CHAI research institutes; (4) CDSS curriculum and degree programs; (5) Industry AI partnerships (Microsoft, Amazon, C3.ai, Lightspeed); (6) AI governance and policy bodies (PAC-AI, CLTC, CITRIS); (7) NSF and federal grants; (8) Gateway Building and CDSS infrastructure; (9) Berkeley RDI and agentic AI developments; (10) Confirmatory searches on key partnership and program details. PILLAR SCORING RATIONALE: P1 Commitment (23/27): Berkeley has robust governance (PAC-AI, CERC-AIR, Berkeley AI Hub, AI Policy Hub, UC Responsible AI Principles), strong executive engagement (Dean Chayes at state and federal AI policy levels, Prof. Mulligan on California Innovation Council), and a centralized AI Hub resource. Deducted 4 points because there is no confirmed standalone executive-level Chief AI Officer position, no published university-wide strategic AI plan document (distinct from the advisory PAC-AI function), and the AI Sandbox remains in beta. P2 Curriculum (26/30): Berkeley earns near-maximum points for CDSS (CS, Data Science, Statistics majors and minors), #1 ranked undergraduate data science major, CS 189 ML course (400+ enrolled), DATASCI 221, an extensive graduate AI curriculum across EECS/iSchool/Haas/Goldman School, and multiple executive AI certificate programs. Deducted 4 points for lack of a confirmed standalone undergraduate AI major or minor and absence of a required AI literacy course across all undergraduates. P3 Partnerships (18/20): Exceptional. Microsoft-BAIR Open Commons, Amazon AI PhD Fellowship (10 Berkeley fellowships), C3.ai DTI ($367M total), Lightspeed-Sky Lab VC partnership ($500K founding), House Fund BAIR Grant ($12M), Governor Newsom Emerging Technology Accelerator, CloudBank 2 NSF co-PI ($20M). Deducted 2 points because no major dedicated corporate AI center physically located on campus (e.g., like a Google or Meta AI research center building on Berkeley grounds) was confirmed. P4 Research (15/16): Extraordinary research depth. BAIR (50+ faculty, 300+ PhD students), CHAI (AI safety, 10th annual workshop 2026), RDI (agentic AI, 40K+ MOOC learners, 2026 summit), Sky Computing Lab (vLLM, open source AI infra), C3.ai DTI, BIDS, CLTC AI Security Initiative, Lawrence Berkeley National Lab affiliation, Simons Institute, Berkeley DeepDrive, BEST Lab. Deducted 1 point because dedicated on-campus AI supercomputing/HPC cluster infrastructure details were not verified (relies on cloud via AWS/Azure agreements and NSF CloudBank). P5 Attitude (6/7): UC Berkeley's posture is firmly enabling and progressive. Multiple governance and responsible AI frameworks coexist with aggressive AI research and commercial translation. Tone from CDSS Dean Chayes, Berkeley AI Hub, RDI, and BAIR is consistently pro-AI with ethics guardrails. Minor deduction for some cautious/neutral tones in faculty AI ethics research and the advisory (non-executive) nature of PAC-AI.","BAIR described as world's most advanced academic AI research lab with 50+ faculty and 300+ PhD students (ai.berkeley.edu/research); CDSS established 2023 as first new college at Berkeley in 50+ years housing #1 ranked data science major (US News); Gateway Building opening Spring 2026 at $550M cost; PAC-AI (Provost's Advisory Council on AI) chaired by CIO Tracy Shinn with Campus AI Sandbox beta since March 2025; Amazon AI PhD Fellowship awarded 10 fellowships to Sky Computing Lab; Microsoft-BAIR Open Commons partnership provides Azure compute; C3.ai DTI $367M investment co-led by UC Berkeley; Lightspeed-Sky Lab first formal VC partnership October 2025 ($500K); House Fund BAIR Grant $12M raised to offset federal cuts (April 2025); Berkeley RDI Agentic AI MOOC at 40,000 global learners and Agentic AI Summit 2026 confirmed; CHAI 2026 Workshop June 4-7, 2026; Governor Newsom Emerging Technology Accelerator formal state-Berkeley partnership (December 2024); Dean Jennifer Chayes co-led California Frontier AI Policy Working Group for Governor; DATASCI 221 Modern AI Strategy course active Spring 2026; CS 189 ML updated Fall 2025 with 400+ students.",,https://ai.berkeley.edu/research

column62: Nearly one-quarter of all suspended NSF grants at UCLA fund computer science, disproportionately affecting AI research capacity [NEW 2026-03-29]","New search confirmed several meaningful developments beyond the existing dataset. (1) OPERATIONAL AI DEPLOYMENTS: BruinChat (launched Aug 28, 2025) is UCLA's AI-powered admissions and financial aid virtual assistant delivering 24/7 multilingual support, with accuracy improving 20% and a 73% increase in conversations since launch. BruinAgent is a separate OARC/DTS pilot AI assistant for campus-wide collaboration. DTS made ChatGPT Edu available at discounted price to faculty and staff in fall 2025. (2) NEW GRANTS/PARTNERSHIPS: Todd Presner's AI and Cultural Heritage Lab received a $308,000 grant from the Claims Conference in March 2026 to study generative AI and Holocaust history. UCLA CTSI and Center for AI and Smart Health launched a Generative AI Discovery and Innovation Seed Grant program with funding start January 4, 2026. (3) INDUSTRY PARTNERSHIP: Coco Robotics named UCLA CS professor Bolei Zhou (60,000+ citations, NSF CAREER Award, MIT TR35 innovator) as Chief AI Scientist to lead a Physical AI Lab (October 2025). (4) CURRICULUM EXPANSION: UCLA Law now has multiple AI-focused courses and an Executive Education Strategic AI for Legal Professionals Program with 7 modules. UCLA TLC funded the AIMS program across introductory STEM courses. (5) DATA GOVERNANCE/POLICY GAP: Daily Bruin published opinion piece February 1, 2026 flagging inconsistency between student AI restrictions and faculty/staff unrestricted access to ChatGPT Edu. (6) FEDERAL FUNDING RISK INTENSIFIED: Partial federal government shutdown began January 30, 2026. Nearly one-quarter of suspended NSF grants fund computer science, disproportionately hitting AI research. NSF suspended the $25M IPAM grant affecting Terence Tao's research. (7) DATAX HIRING: UCLA Mathematics conducting a DataX-sponsored tenure-track faculty search for 2026-27 appointment, suggesting some hiring continues as exemption from broader freeze. SCORING RATIONALE: P1 held at 25 (strong multi-level governance and C-suite leadership confirmed; BruinChat and BruinAgent as operational AI deployments add depth; however post-June 2025 governance transition still being operationalized and policy inconsistency flagged). P2 held at 25 (UCLA Law AI curriculum additions, AIMS program, and confirmed 67% student GenAI usage all add positive evidence; however no mandatory undergrad AI literacy requirement and no standalone AI degree remain gaps). P3 held at 18 (Coco Robotics Physical AI Lab with Bolei Zhou adds new industry partnership evidence; CTSI AI seed grant adds internal funding mechanism; deducting for lack of mega-consortium anchor comparable to Stanford). P4 held at 14 (confirmed 68 pilot projects across 27 departments; BruinChat operational deployment with measurable outcomes; federal funding disruption and hiring freeze remain structural constraints). P5 held at 5 (operational BruinChat and BruinAgent deployments confirm AI operationalization in student-facing services; governance gap flagged by Daily Bruin prevents higher score).",Dual C-suite AI leadership (CDAO Mattmann + Health CAO Lukac); first California university ChatGPT Enterprise adoption with 68 confirmed pilots across 27 departments; BruinChat operational AI deployment with 73% conversation increase; Amazon Science Hub with 50 doctoral fellowships; UCLA CS #3 AI ranking CSRankings.org; $584M federal grant risk with hiring freeze directly impeding DataX 60-faculty goal; 67% student GenAI adoption rate; Coco Robotics Physical AI Lab partnership with Bolei Zhou; NSF IPAM $25M grant suspension affecting Terence Tao mathematical AI research,,https://dts.ucla.edu/initiatives/ai/governance

column39: UCSD-specific AI governance committee (distinct from UC-system AI Council) not confirmed; campus relies on UC-system governance body","Comprehensive first-time research conducted March 29, 2026. P1 COMMITMENT (21/27): UCSD has strong AI leadership signals including an inaugural Chief Health AI Officer (Dr. Karandeep Singh), Chancellor Khosla's direct sponsorship of TritonGPT and the AI major, UC system-level AI governance (Presidential Working Group, AI Council, Responsible AI Principles with UCSD faculty involvement), a 2024 Senate-Administration GenAI in Education Final Report with 5 formal recommendations, and a campus-wide AI technology support portal (blink.ucsd.edu/technology/ai). The new SCIDS school (Regents-approved July 2024) with founding Dean Rajesh Gupta demonstrates structural commitment. Deductions: no standalone campus CAIO at EVC level, no published standalone UCSD AI strategic plan document. P2 CURRICULUM (27/30): UCSD launched the B.S. in Artificial Intelligence in Fall 2025 (first 200+ students, targeted 1,000 by 2029). The program includes mandatory ethics course, two new foundational AI courses (CSE 25, CSE 55), 12 units of AI electives, and a senior capstone. HDSI offers B.S., M.S., and Ph.D. in Data Science. Cross-disciplinary AI integration is extensive (AI+medicine, AI+climate, AI+bioinformatics, AI+policy). Google.org-funded GenAI CS Education Consortium offers 6 turnkey courses. TritonGPT Instructional AI Pilot launched Fall 2025. Extended Studies offers Responsible AI and AI Fundamentals for Leadership courses. Deduction: AI major access restricted in 2025-26 ramp-up phase. P3 PARTNERSHIPS (16/20): TILOS AI Institute with Intel co-sponsorship; Google.org partnership for GenAI CS Education Consortium; Qualcomm Technologies co-sponsored San Diego Wireless Summit on AI; NVIDIA gifted DGX B200 and AMD gifted Mi350x to Hao AI Lab; Virbela AI simulation partnership via Qualcomm Institute; Qualcomm Institute has ~200 industry partners; AIM 2026 cohort with DARPA and Air Force Research Lab linkages; Horizon Accelerator with Salesforce advisors. No confirmed named corporate AI center physically on campus. P4 RESEARCH (15/16): TILOS NSF AI Institute ($20M, UCSD-led with MIT, Penn, UT Austin, Yale, Intel); NSF EnCORE Institute at UCSD; HDSI (51 faculty, 15 postdocs, 4,800 students); SDSC (national HPC center, part of SCIDS); Hao AI Lab with NVIDIA DGX B200 and AMD Mi350x, 8 top-tier papers in 2025 (ICLR, ICML, NeurIPS, ACL, TPAMI); MLSys group; GAIA Initiative (28+ faculty); UCSD AI faculty hiring search for AI and Public Policy. Compute infrastructure is world-class (SDSC supercomputer + gifted NVIDIA/AMD hardware). Deduction: no confirmed NAIRR pilot site or DOE AI research designation. P5 ATTITUDE (6/7): UC San Diego's overall posture is highly progressive and enabling -- Chancellor actively champions AI, TritonGPT is proactive internal deployment, new school and new degree program launched, multiple new AI initiatives in 2025 (GAIA, GenAI Consortium, Instructional AI Pilot), and research papers adopted by major industry players. One minor deduction for no single campus AI strategic vision document.","B.S. AI major launched Fall 2025 (200+ students, 1,000 target by 2029); SCIDS school approved July 2024 merging HDSI and SDSC; TritonGPT deployed to 38,000+ campus users hosted on SDSC, licensed to 3+ other institutions; TILOS NSF AI Institute ($20M, UCSD-led); NVIDIA DGX B200 gifted to Hao AI Lab Dec 2025; GAIA Initiative launched Dec 2025 (28+ faculty, NSF STC application planned); Google.org partnership for GenAI CS Education Consortium; Chief Health AI Officer appointed; mandatory AI ethics course in new curriculum; EnCORE NSF Institute; Qualcomm Technologies AI-wireless summit co-sponsorship; UC Responsible AI Principles with UCSD faculty on Presidential Working Group.",,"https://ucsd.edu/research-innovation/artificial-intelligence.html

column52: Federal research funding headwinds noted in State of Campus 2026 -- NSF down 3%, NIH up only 1%, with indirect cost pressure potentially constraining AI research investment [NEW 2026-03-29]","NEW EVIDENCE FOUND (2026-03-29): (1) State of Campus address (Feb 26, 2026) and March 2026 Aggie editorial board interview with Chancellor May both confirm that an AI Steering Committee has been convened, co-chaired by CIDSE Aisha Jackson and Academic Senate Chair Kadee Russ, with representation from four Senate committees. This is a structural governance evolution from the AI Council -- it moves AI oversight into a standing joint Senate-Administration body. (2) The Aggie AI tool suite has materially expanded: Google Gemini and NotebookLM are now available at no cost to all students, faculty, and staff through the UC Davis Google Workspace. ChatGPT Edu is available for purchase by faculty and staff. Rocky chatbot expanded to UC Davis Health employees as of January 20, 2026, and a Rocky-in-Canvas pilot launched Winter 2026. An AI pilot of the SiteFarm campus CMS launched January 26, 2026. (3) UC Davis Health has a dedicated Chief AI Adviser (Dennis Chornenky, appointed June 2023), a robust clinical AI governance structure with the Analytics Oversight Committee (AOC) using SAFE/SMART frameworks, and a Health Data Oversight Committee (HDOC) with four subcommittees -- this is a mature, institutionalized health AI governance stack. In March 2026, the first Emergency Medicine resident AI scribe pilot was launched and vetted by the oversight committee. (4) AICE Industry Consortium was formally introduced at the AI Innovation Showcase (October 16, 2025), attended by 100+ industry partners, alumni, and faculty, with Race Capital as sponsor -- this is a new formal mechanism for AI industry partnership. (5) The Resnick Center for Agricultural Innovation is on track to open fall/summer 2026 and will include dedicated flexible labs for AI, big data, and robotics. A $25M philanthropic bequest (February 2026) funds three new endowed chairs in biological and agricultural engineering and includes naming of the C.L. Best Agricultural Innovation Robotics and Sensing Suite. (6) UC Davis and University College Dublin launched their 2025-2026 UCD Squared joint research fund in March 2026, with AI and One Health as central themes -- multiple AI research projects funded. (7) Chancellor acknowledged growing AI academic misconduct cases as a concern in March 2026. (8) CIDSE role confirmed as a two-year term, introducing succession risk. SCORING RATIONALE: P1 raised from 22 to 24: AI Steering Committee (joint Senate-Administration, co-chaired by CIDSE + Senate Chair) is a significant governance formalization step beyond the AI Council alone; plus confirmed portfolio of AI tools and investment commitment from State of Campus 2026. Still no standalone CAIO or dedicated AI strategic plan document, capping the score. P2 stays at 23: No new degree programs found, but Gemini/NotebookLM/Rocky Canvas pilot represent expanded AI learning infrastructure. P3 raised from 17 to 17 (no change -- already high): AICE Industry Consortium is new but still early-stage; UCD Squared AI partnership adds international dimension. Score maintained as no major new corporate named AI center or large new industry grant confirmed. P4 raised from 14 to 15: Resnick Center AI/robotics labs opening 2026 adds new physical AI research infrastructure; $25M bequest adds faculty chairs with AI focus; UC Davis Health AI research adds to research output evidence. P5 raised from 6 to 6 (no change): UC Davis posture is strongly enabling and progressive -- Chancellor personally advocates for AI in healthcare, State of Campus confirms AI investment commitment, deliberative governance process in place. Capped at 6 (not 7) because governance is still being finalized and some tools remain in pilot phase.","State of Campus 2026 (Feb 26, 2026) confirms AI Steering Committee co-chaired by CIDSE Aisha Jackson and Academic Senate Chair Kadee Russ; Aggie AI now includes Google Gemini and NotebookLM at no cost for all users; Rocky expanded to Health employees and Canvas LMS pilot; AICE Industry Consortium launched Oct 2025; Resnick Center for Agricultural Innovation (opening 2026) includes dedicated AI and robotics labs; UC Davis Health Chief AI Adviser Chornenky and AOC/HDOC clinical AI governance stack operational; UCD Squared 2025-2026 AI and One Health joint research fund launched March 2026; $25M ag-tech bequest includes AI/robotics suite.",,https://leadership.ucdavis.edu/about/councils-committees-boards/artificial-intelligence

column33: UCI does not appear on lists of NSF National AI Research Institute awardees as a lead institution","COMPREHENSIVE FIRST-TIME RESEARCH as of 2026-03-29. All evidence is new. P1 COMMITMENT (21/27): UCI has a named Director of AI (Sarkis Daglian, OIT), a Chief Digital Officer (Tom Andriola, VC for Data and IT), and the Bren ICS Dean actively championing AI. The HR division publicly discussed AI governance and best practices in Dec 2025. UCI participates in the UC system AI Council. ZotGPT itself reflects a university-level commitment operationalized through OIT with an AI-specific sub-team. However, no single consolidated AI strategic plan document was found published at the university level, and there is no Vice Provost or VP for AI title. The AI leadership is distributed across OIT, individual schools, and institutes rather than through a dedicated AI Office. Score reflects strong operational commitment with partial governance formalization. P2 CURRICULUM (24/30): UCI has an exceptionally rich and growing AI curriculum portfolio. Active programs include: MS Computer Science with AI/ML concentration (Bren ICS), Master of Computer Science with AI focus (MCS, Bren ICS), Master of Data Science (Bren ICS), MES-AILA from School of Education (online, 12-month), and the newly announced Master of Applied AI for Science (Physical Sciences, cohort fall 2026). The Bren School is the only standalone ICS school in the UC system. AI is integrated into nursing (ZotGPT patient simulations), business (Merage), law (Noyce Initiative), and physical sciences curricula. No standalone undergraduate AI major was found, only concentrations. Score docked slightly for lack of undergrad AI major and for the Applied AI for Science program not yet being operational. P3 PARTNERSHIPS (16/20): Strong corporate and government partnership evidence. SAP donated $2M to endow the Hasso Plattner Chair in AI (March 2025). HPI (Hasso Plattner Institute, Germany) maintains an active ML/Data Science Research Center at UCI with 16 PhD fellows. UCOP funded the $6M Geophysicist.AI initiative led by UCI (June 2025). UCI faculty receive AI grants from NSF, DARPA, IARPA, DOE, NIH, Amazon, Allen AI, Adobe, Intel, Qualcomm, Disney. The Samueli School has a Corporate Partners Program. AI@UCI student club collaborated with AWS. Merage Center for Digital Transformation hosted major industry conferences (DLA 2025, 2026). No single dominant on-campus physical AI lab from a Big Tech partner (e.g., Amazon Science hub, Google AI center) found. P4 RESEARCH (13/16): Multiple active AI research institutes: AISI (AI in Science Institute), CML (Center for Machine Learning and Intelligent Systems), ProperAI/Engineering+ Society Institute (launched summer 2025, $2.5M, 2025-2030), HPI ML/Data Science Center, Irvine Initiative in AI Law and Society, ProperData NSF SATC Frontiers ($10M). Distinguished AI faculty include Pierre Baldi (AISI Director), Erik Sudderth (CML Director), Stephan Mandt (Chan Zuckerberg AI Resident, NSF CAREER, ETH Zurich Guest Professor, NeurIPS Outstanding Paper 2023), Sameer Singh (DARPA Riser, multiple best paper awards), and many others funded by NSF/DARPA/NIH/DOE. High-performance computing capabilities cited. UCI maintains the international UCI ML Repository. Score docked slightly because no confirmed NSF National AI Research Institute award as lead institution was found, and exact compute infrastructure specs were not publicly detailed. P5 ATTITUDE (6/7): UCI's posture is clearly progressive/enabling. It was first UC campus to give campus-wide free AI access to all students. It deployed ZotGPT with 10+ frontier models, launched an AI Academy, deployed AI simulations in health care education, encouraged 1,000+ custom AI bots, and is openly exploring agentic AI. Leadership language is consistently enabling with explicit equity framing. One point withheld because federated governance concerns and a cautious tone around agentic AI risks were noted alongside the enabling posture.","ZotGPT: first UC campus to give all students unrestricted free AI access (April 2024); 10+ frontier models as of 2026; 11,000+ student users; 100,000+ messages per quarter. column27: Federal funding environment uncertain in 2026 with reported UCSB hiring freeze due to Trump administration cuts [NEW 2026-03-29]","This is a first-time AIREDEX research entry for UC Santa Barbara as of March 29, 2026. Key findings by pillar: P1 COMMITMENT -- New Chancellor Dennis Assanis (took office Sept. 1, 2025) publicly endorsed the BS in AI proposal. The EVC and Provost office announced on January 5, 2026 the formation of a joint Senate-Administration AI Advisory Committee co-chaired by CS Prof. Timothy Sherwood and Film/Media Prof. Lisa Parks, tasked with developing principles and policies for ethical AI use in teaching, research, and administration. The CIO office (Josh Bright, Associate Vice Chancellor for IT and CIO) chairs an ITC AI Subcommittee and has published AI use guidelines. An AI Community of Practice is run out of the Office of Teaching and Learning. No dedicated AI office or CAIO role was identified; no campus-wide multi-year AI strategic plan document was found. Governance structures exist but are distributed rather than centralized. P2 CURRICULUM -- The most significant 2026 development is the UCSB Academic Senate's January 2026 near-unanimous approval of a Bachelor of Science in Artificial Intelligence, the first standalone undergraduate AI degree at UCSB, launching Fall 2026 in the CS Department. Curriculum covers AI Foundations (computing/AI principles, stats, optimization), Domain-Specific (ML, NLP, Computer Vision, Deep Learning), and a third area. The proposal was endorsed by Chancellor Assanis. Students have access to CRML and the ACTION Institute. Initial enrollment ~20 freshmen, scaling to ~200 by 2029. UCSB Professional and Continuing Education offers Applied AI and Innovation certificates (Degree+ for undergrads and professional track). Cross-disciplinary AI integration is evidenced by CCS Winter 2026 AI Special Topics course, HAX Lab courses, and HUML. No AI-specific minor was identified in searches, though AI coursework is woven into CS, ECE, and other programs. P3 PARTNERSHIPS -- Google Quantum AI is physically located in Goleta with deep historical UCSB ties; the relationship involves curriculum input, shared use of the UCSB NanoFab facility, and talent pipelines. The CRML Agentic AI Summit January 2026 brought in industry sponsors including ScOp VC, ChipAgents (founded by CRML's William Wang), Simular, and Megagon Labs. The ACTION NSF Institute actively collaborates with DHS and industry partners for technology transfer and graduate internships. $750K Schmidt Sciences Humanities and AI grant secured. UCSB Corporate Programs and Office of Technology and Industry Alliances serve as formal industry gateway. No single dominant named corporate AI center (e.g., Amazon, Microsoft, or Meta on-campus lab) was identified. P4 RESEARCH -- Multiple active AI research entities: (1) NSF AI Institute ACTION ($20M, 5-year, led by Vigna/Kruegel/Hespanha/Singh) for AI-powered cybersecurity; (2) Center for Responsible Machine Learning (CRML), directed by Mellichamp Professor William Wang, active in NLP, LLMs, responsible AI, with 2025-2026 publications at ICML, ICLR, NeurIPS; (3) Mellichamp Mind and Machine Intelligence Initiative (endowed, cross-disciplinary); (4) Center for Humanities and Machine Learning (HUML) for critical AI; (5) REAL AI for Science (ECE, AI-powered digital twins); (6) HAX Lab (Human-AI interaction, papers at ICLR 2026, IUI 2026); (7) Center for Multimodal Big Data Science and Healthcare with NSF-funded BisQue Deep Learning CI project. Faculty include William Wang (Mellichamp Prof. AI, NLP/LLMs), Yao Qin (hallucination detection, AI healthcare, ICLR 2026 paper), Shiyu Chang (ML/NLP/CV, publications at ICML 2025, ICLR 2025), Giovanni Vigna (AI cybersecurity), B.S. Manjunath (computer vision, AI). HPC infrastructure: UCSB Center for Scientific Computing at CNSI with Braid2 condo cluster (CPU+GPU nodes), ITS Supercomputing with TSCC and NSF ACCESS access. No dedicated large-scale AI training cluster was identified. P5 ATTITUDE -- Overall posture is enabling/progressive. The institution is moving decisively with a new undergraduate degree, formal governance committee, active research institutes, industry events, and cross-disciplinary integration. The new chancellor endorsed AI curriculum expansion. The AI CoP and CIO guidelines reflect a responsible-innovation stance. One note: the Daily Nexus article about replacing students with AI (July 2025) is clearly campus satire and was excluded from scoring. Federal funding uncertainty and a reported hiring freeze represent headwinds.","BS in AI approved January 2026 (launching Fall 2026, endorsed by Chancellor Assanis); Joint Senate-Admin AI Advisory Committee formed January 5, 2026 by EVC/Provost office; NSF ACTION Institute $20M AI cybersecurity institute led by UCSB; CRML directed by Mellichamp Professor of AI William Wang; Mellichamp Mind and Machine Initiative (endowed); HAX Lab ICLR 2026 paper; Inaugural UCSB Agentic AI Summit January 2026 with industry sponsors; Google Quantum AI in Goleta with deep UCSB ties; $750K Schmidt Sciences AI grant; BisQue Deep Learning NSF cyberinfrastructure grant; UCSB Professional and Continuing Education Applied AI and Innovation certificate; CIO ITC AI Subcommittee with published AI use guidelines.",,https://news.ucsb.edu/2026/022400/ucsb-computer-science-department-offer-undergraduate-major-ai
